family_manifest.population: People with a pigmented lesion under review; children, pregnancy, previous melanoma, familial syndromes, and immunosuppression need specialist context.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE primary and specialist dermatology or skin-cancer assessment.

family_manifest.population: Adults or children with a suspected pityriasis-rosea-type eruption; pregnancy, medication, infection, mucosal, systemic, and atypical contexts require separate evidence.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Primary and outpatient dermatology assessment.

family_manifest.population: Adults or children with a clinician-suspected or established plantar wart; diabetes, neuropathy, poor perfusion, immunosuppression, pregnancy, and diagnostic uncertainty require qualified assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE primary, podiatry, and dermatology review.

family_manifest.population: Adults or children returning after a skin biopsy; procedure type, site, closure, bleeding risk, immune status, pathology, and infection context require individual review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE primary, dermatology, surgery, pathology, and wound-care review.

family_manifest.population: Adults or children with a pressure-prone skin area or pressure-injury concern; mobility, neurologic, nutritional, device, critical-care, pediatric, and palliative contexts need qualified assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE home, primary, community, emergency, inpatient, rehabilitation, and wound-care review.

family_manifest.population: Primarily adults with chronic itch and no obvious primary rash; acute, pediatric, pregnancy, older-person, systemic, neurologic, psychiatric, medicine, and renal or hepatic contexts need qualified review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE primary, dermatology, and multidisciplinary assessment.

family_manifest.population: Adults or children with clinician-established or suspected psoriasis reporting worsening; pregnancy, systemic illness, joint symptoms, pediatric, and medicine contexts need specific assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE primary and dermatology assessment.

family_manifest.population: Partial: exact for pediatric broad-rash history and atopic-dermatitis-compatible findings; adult broad rash and many alternative diagnoses are not covered.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Direct for DHA telehealth in the stated pediatric or atopic contexts; partial for broad in-person dermatology assessment.

family_manifest.population: Adults with clinician-established or suspected rosacea reporting worsening; ocular, pregnancy, medicine, and diagnostic-uncertainty contexts require separate review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE primary, dermatology, and ophthalmology-linked assessment.

family_manifest.population: Adults or children with close contact or possible exposure; institutions, outbreaks, infants, pregnancy, older people, immunosuppression, and crusted disease need qualified review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE community, primary, school, care-home, hospital, infection-control, and dermatology assessment.

family_manifest.population: Infants, adolescents, or adults with scalp scale; psoriasis, tinea, contact, hair loss, infection, and immune contexts need differential review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE primary, telehealth, pediatric, and dermatology assessment with direct examination when needed.

family_manifest.population: Adults or children with a skin lump considered to be a cyst; acute inflammation, infection, atypical lesion, recurrence, and immune context require separate assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Primary care, dermatology, and minor-surgery assessment.

family_manifest.population: Partial: exact for guideline-described infant, adolescent, and adult seborrheic dermatitis; every scalp, facial, fold, infectious, psoriatic, fungal, or immune-related condition is not fully covered.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Direct for DHA telehealth history, appearance, red-flag, differential, and clinician-led management documentation; diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain clinician-led.

family_manifest.population: People under clinician-directed surveillance after melanoma or another skin cancer; cancer type, stage, treatment, age, immune status, hereditary risk, and local protocol require individual review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE dermatology, oncology, surgery, and primary-care surveillance shared care.

family_manifest.population: Adults with lower-limb eczema or pigmentation considered in a venous context; arterial disease, diabetes, ulcer, infection, heart failure, pregnancy, and mobility context need separate assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE primary, dermatology, vascular, and wound-care review.

family_manifest.population: Adults or children with ultraviolet or heat-associated skin symptoms; blistering, large surface area, infant, pregnancy, medicine, heat illness, and systemic context require separate evidence.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Community, primary, pediatric, dermatology, and emergency assessment.

family_manifest.population: Partial: exact for suspected ringworm or dermatophyte infection and tinea-corporis features; every annular rash, scalp disease, nail disease, bacterial complication, or immune-status qualifier is not fully covered.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Partial for outpatient recognition and clinician-selected testing; diagnosis, treatment, medicine, laboratory selection, and referral remain clinician-led.

family_manifest.population: Adults or children with clinician-established or suspected urticaria returning for review; angioedema, airway, anaphylaxis, pregnancy, pediatric, inducible, medicine, and chronic-disease context require qualified assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE primary, allergy, dermatology, pediatric, and emergency-linked assessment.

family_manifest.population: Adults or children with a purpuric or vasculitic-appearing rash; systemic, renal, gastrointestinal, neurologic, infectious, medicine, pediatric, and pregnancy contexts require urgent qualified assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Primary, dermatology, rheumatology, nephrology, pediatric, and emergency assessment.

family_manifest.population: Adults with lower-limb eczema considered in a venous context; arterial disease, diabetes, ulcer, infection, heart failure, pregnancy, and mobility context need separate assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE primary, dermatology, vascular, and wound-care review.

family_manifest.population: Adults or children with clinician-suspected or established vitiligo; rapid progression, diagnostic uncertainty, autoimmune disease, thyroid, pregnancy, child, and psychosocial context need qualified review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE primary and specialist dermatology assessment.

family_manifest.population: Adults or children with a clinician-suspected or established non-genital cutaneous wart; diabetes, poor perfusion, immunosuppression, pregnancy, genital location, and diagnostic uncertainty require separate assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: UAE primary, pediatric, podiatry, and dermatology follow-up.

family_manifest.population: Direct for plantar warts or verrucae; general, periungual, facial, and genital warts are outside the exact source scope.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Supports primary and dermatology lesion history and examination documentation only.

family_manifest.population: Partial: exact for surgical and postoperative wounds; traumatic wounds, scar review, suture removal, and every wound subtype are not fully covered.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Exact for postoperative, community, and specialist wound care; partial for telehealth-only or traumatic-wound assessment.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED abdominal pain documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults with undifferentiated abdominal pain. Pediatric, pregnancy-related, traumatic, postoperative, older or frail, bleeding, urological and pelvic presentations require their own qualified assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: DHA guidance is for virtual care; only documentation domains transferable to UAE emergency assessment were mapped.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED admission documentation
family_manifest.population: Any adult or child for whom an emergency clinician is considering or has decided admission; condition, age and service-specific requirements vary.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency-to-inpatient transition in UAE facilities.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED allergic reaction documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children with a suspected allergic reaction; exact support is strongest for suspected medicine-related reactions, while food, venom, contact and idiopathic reactions remain less directly covered.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency documentation and subsequent allergy-record communication.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED altered mental status documentation
family_manifest.population: Exact evidence applies to adults aged 18 and over in hospital who may have delirium indicators; children and non-delirium causes require separate evidence.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Acute hospital evidence is partially transferable to the emergency department; long-term-care recommendations were not mapped.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED anaphylaxis documentation-only
family_manifest.population: Adults and children with clinician-suspected anaphylaxis; pregnancy, infants, comorbidity and refractory reactions require qualified review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency recognition and documentation; prehospital and post-event pathways require local adaptation.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED asthma exacerbation documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children with clinician-suspected asthma exacerbation; very young children, pregnancy and major comorbidity need separate review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: DHA virtual-care domains partially transfer to UAE emergency documentation; emergency treatment and in-person assessment remain local.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED back pain documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults with acute low back pain; pediatric, pregnancy, trauma, postoperative and non-lumbar presentations require separate evidence.
family_manifest.intended_setting: DHA virtual-care history and red-flag domains partially transfer to emergency documentation; in-person examination and acute pathways remain local.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED blood test result documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults or children whose ED-requested pathology result is reviewed; the clinical meaning depends on the individual presentation and test.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency department result-review governance and communication.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED burn documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children with thermal, chemical, electrical or other burn injury; inhalation, major burns, safeguarding, pregnancy and comorbidity require qualified emergency review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: DHA virtual guidance domains partially transfer to in-person UAE emergency documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED capacity discussion documentation
family_manifest.population: Patients whose capacity for a specific decision at a specific time is questioned; pediatric consent, guardianship, emergencies and mental-health law need separate qualified review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: All clinical settings, including emergency care, but legal application is jurisdiction-specific.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED chest pain documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults with chest pain; pediatric, pregnancy, trauma, postoperative and established-diagnosis pathways require separate evidence.
family_manifest.intended_setting: DHA virtual-care documentation domains partially transfer to UAE emergency assessment.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED COPD exacerbation documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults with clinician-established COPD and a possible acute exacerbation; asthma, heart failure, pneumonia, pulmonary embolism and other mimics require separate assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: NICE covers community and hospital COPD care; only symptom-change documentation is mapped to ED use.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED critical incident documentation
family_manifest.population: Any patient involved in a critical clinical incident; exact content varies by incident, harm, legal status and facility policy.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency clinical records and separate organisational incident-reporting systems.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED death verification documentation-only
family_manifest.population: A deceased adult or child; expected, unexpected, traumatic, forensic, pediatric and infectious deaths have different requirements.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency department or hospital verification before applicable certification and notification processes.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED diarrhea documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults with acute diarrhea; children, pregnancy, chronic diarrhea, inflammatory bowel disease, immunocompromise and recent surgery require separate assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: DHA virtual-care documentation domains partially transfer to UAE emergency assessment.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED discharge documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children discharged directly from an ED; condition-specific, safeguarding, mental-health and high-risk discharge requirements may add obligations.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency department discharge communication and handover to primary care.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED dizziness documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults with dizziness; children, pregnancy, trauma, intoxication and major neurologic or cardiovascular presentations require separate assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: DHA virtual-care domains partially transfer to UAE ED documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED domestic violence screening documentation
family_manifest.population: WHO evidence primarily addresses women experiencing intimate-partner or sexual violence; men, people under 18, elders, dependent adults and non-partner violence require separate evidence.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Health-care and emergency encounters where disclosure or clinician suspicion exists.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED ear nose throat presentation
family_manifest.population: Adults or children with an undifferentiated ear, nose or throat complaint; airway, bleeding, infection, trauma and foreign-body contexts differ materially.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency assessment with condition-specific ENT pathways.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED ECG documentation
family_manifest.population: Any adult or child for whom an ECG was performed; age, indication, rhythm and clinical context vary.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency ECG acquisition and clinician interpretation documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED eye injury documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children with possible eye injury; exact support is strongest for corneal abrasion and urgent anterior-eye presentations.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Urgent eye assessment and referral principles partially transfer to emergency documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED fall documentation
family_manifest.population: NICE exact evidence covers people 65+ and higher-risk people 50–64; WHO trauma domains broaden mechanism assessment but do not provide a complete pediatric or younger-adult falls pathway.
family_manifest.intended_setting: NICE explicitly includes emergency departments in community settings; WHO BEC supports emergency trauma assessment.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED family update documentation
family_manifest.population: Patients for whom a family or representative update is considered; consent, capacity, age, guardianship and confidentiality vary.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency communication with family, carers or legal representatives.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED fever documentation
family_manifest.population: Broad fever workflow spanning adults and children; NICE sepsis evidence applies when sepsis is suspected and DHA evidence applies to pediatric fever, leaving many non-sepsis adult presentations incompletely covered.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency assessment; DHA virtual pediatric evidence only partially transfers to in-person ED care.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED fracture suspicion documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children with suspected non-complex fracture; open, pelvic, spinal, skull, pathological and major-trauma injuries need separate pathways.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency assessment of suspected non-complex fracture.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED handover documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children receiving emergency care; WHO BEC evidence is strongest for acute illness or trauma and does not define every interdepartmental handover field.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency transfer, referral and disposition documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED headache documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults with headache; children, pregnancy/postpartum, trauma and established neurological disease require separate assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: DHA virtual-care domains partially transfer to UAE emergency documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED imaging result documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults or children whose ED-requested imaging report is reviewed; applicability depends on modality, anatomy and presentation.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency department radiology-report review, communication, responsibility and follow-up.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED interpreter documentation
family_manifest.population: Patients requiring language or communication support; age, capacity, disability, safeguarding and confidentiality alter requirements.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency communication and documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED intoxication documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults or children with suspected alcohol, medicine, drug or mixed intoxication; pregnancy, trauma, safeguarding and withdrawal differ materially.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency medical and mental-health assessment with toxicology support as needed.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED laceration documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children with an open wound or laceration; bites, puncture, tendon, nerve, vascular, facial, hand, genital and contaminated wounds need additional expertise.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency trauma and wound assessment.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED medication administration documentation
family_manifest.population: Any adult or child receiving a medicine in emergency care; requirements vary by medicine, route, indication, age and monitoring.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency medication administration and electronic medication records.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED mental health presentation documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children with a primary mental-health presentation; age-specific, neurodevelopmental, intoxication, medical and safeguarding contexts require qualified review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency mental-health triage, assessment and liaison working.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED minor head injury documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children with possible minor head injury; anticoagulation, infancy, older age, seizure, penetrating injury and polytrauma require additional assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: DHA virtual-care domains partially transfer to UAE emergency in-person assessment.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED observation-unit review
family_manifest.population: Adults and children under ED observation; WHO BEC evidence is strongest for acute illness or trauma and is not a complete observation-unit standard.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency observation and reassessment before clinician disposition.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED overdose documentation-only
family_manifest.population: NICE and RCEM exact mappings apply mainly to overdose in a self-harm context; accidental, recreational, occupational and pediatric ingestions remain incompletely covered.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency physical and mental-health assessment after self-poisoning.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED patient declined care documentation
family_manifest.population: Patients declining assessment, treatment, admission, transfer or follow-up; children, impaired capacity, emergencies and mental-health law require specific review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency decision-making and documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED pediatric fever documentation
family_manifest.population: Children with fever; neonates, immunocompromised children and children with complex conditions require urgent age- and condition-specific review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: DHA virtual pediatric assessment partially transfers to UAE ED documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED pelvic pain documentation
family_manifest.population: RCOG evidence covers women with chronic pelvic pain; NICE evidence covers possible early-pregnancy complications. Acute non-pregnancy, pediatric, male and postoperative pelvic pain remain incompletely covered.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency pelvic-pain assessment with gynecology or early-pregnancy pathways when applicable.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED pregnancy bleeding documentation
family_manifest.population: People with bleeding in early pregnancy; later gestation, postpartum bleeding, trauma and non-pregnancy bleeding require different pathways.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency and early-pregnancy assessment services.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED procedural sedation documentation prompt
family_manifest.population: Adults and children excluding neonates undergoing clinician-decided ED procedural sedation; pregnancy, older age and major comorbidity require additional review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency procedural sedation by credentialed teams with local governance.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED procedure note documentation prompt
family_manifest.population: Adults and children undergoing an invasive or high-risk ED procedure; procedure-specific contraindications and competencies remain separate.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency procedures performed by qualified staff under local policy.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED reattendance documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults or children returning after a recent emergency visit; timing, reason, previous outcome and current acuity vary.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency reassessment after prior attendance.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED referral documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults or children for whom an ED clinician has already decided or discussed referral; specialty and urgency vary.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency referral, transfer and discharge handover.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED renal colic documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults, children or pregnant people with suspected or confirmed renal/ureteric stones; the imaging and management pathway is population-specific.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency and urology assessment; exact mappings are limited to already performed/reviewed results and clinician-entered follow-up.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED safeguarding documentation
family_manifest.population: Exact evidence covers children/young people and adult women experiencing IPV; vulnerable adults, men, elders, trafficking and other safeguarding contexts remain incompletely covered.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency recognition, assessment and documentation of safeguarding concern.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED safety-net documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children discharged or managed with clinician-confirmed safety-net discussion; condition-specific warning features differ.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency discharge and transition communication.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED seizure documentation
family_manifest.population: Children, young people and adults with a suspected or confirmed seizure; pregnancy, neonates, status, intoxication and acute symptomatic causes require additional pathways.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency initial seizure assessment and communication.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED self-harm documentation prompts
family_manifest.population: Children, young people and adults after self-harm; age, method, physical injury, intoxication, neurodevelopmental, safeguarding and legal context require qualified review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency physical and psychosocial assessment after self-harm.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED shortness of breath documentation
family_manifest.population: Exact chronic-source coverage is limited to adults with persistent breathlessness; WHO material adds broad acute-care documentation but not a breathlessness-specific diagnostic pathway. Paediatric, pregnancy or postpartum, postoperative, traumatic, frail and immunocompromised presentations require separate review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency documentation with partial support from a chronic primary-care pathway and general first-contact emergency guidance.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED sprain documentation
family_manifest.population: People with sprain- or strain-compatible injuries suitable for assessment; open injury, major trauma, neurovascular compromise, infection and fracture concern require distinct pathways.
family_manifest.intended_setting: DHA telehealth evidence is direct for Dubai virtual care and partial for in-person emergency documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED syncope documentation
family_manifest.population: People aged 16 years and older with suspected transient loss of consciousness; paediatric syncope and non-TLoC falls require separate evidence.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Initial and emergency assessment of suspected transient loss of consciousness.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED testicular pain documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults or children with acute or chronic testicular pain; age, trauma, infection, torsion, hernia and referred pain contexts differ materially.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency assessment of undifferentiated testicular pain.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED transfer documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children in first-contact emergency care when transfer has already been assessed or arranged.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency referral and interfacility handover; WHO BEC is broad international guidance.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED triage documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children presenting to first-contact emergency care; neonates, pregnancy and specialty presentations may require separate triage pathways.
family_manifest.intended_setting: First-contact emergency triage documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED urinary symptoms documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults or children with dysuria, frequency, urgency, retention, haematuria, incontinence or other urinary symptoms; sex, pregnancy and systemic illness change the pathway.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Emergency assessment of undifferentiated urinary symptoms.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED violence/aggression incident documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults, children or young people involved in violence or aggression; age, mental state, intoxication, injury, pregnancy and setting alter requirements.
family_manifest.intended_setting: NICE includes emergency-department involvement but much of the detailed source is mental-health inpatient guidance, so the mapping is partial.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED vomiting documentation
family_manifest.population: People with nausea or vomiting; the DHA applicability section does not state a precise age range and pregnancy, paediatric, postoperative and immunocompromised presentations require added review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Dubai telehealth evidence with partial applicability to in-person emergency assessment.

family_manifest.family_name: Emergency Medicine documentation-only — ED wound documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults and children with open wounds or lacerations; bites, burns, penetrating injuries, major trauma, surgical wounds and high-risk hosts require distinct evidence.
family_manifest.intended_setting: First-contact emergency wound assessment.

family_manifest.population: Partial: age, pregnancy, frailty, lesion, hormonal, and malignancy-risk qualifiers are not fully represented in the legacy workflow.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Partial: exact for incidentaloma assessment and qualified follow-up, not diagnosis, surgery, or treatment.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — B12 deficiency endocrine review
family_manifest.population: People aged 16 years and older; paediatric cases and condition-specific pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, nitrous-oxide and neurological contexts require qualified review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Primary or endocrine follow-up of suspected or confirmed B12 deficiency.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Bone density result review
family_manifest.population: Primarily adults over 50 years in the DoH screening population; younger adults, children, pregnancy and disease-specific bone disorders require separate evidence.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Abu Dhabi primary-care screening and clinician-led bone-density result review.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Calcium result review
family_manifest.population: People with calcium findings that may indicate primary hyperparathyroidism; paediatric, pregnancy, renal, oncology, acute severe and hypocalcaemia contexts require separate evidence.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Primary or endocrine review of a calcium result, with disease-specific partial evidence.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Cold intolerance endocrine documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults or children reporting cold intolerance; thyroid, anaemia, medication, environmental, nutritional and other contexts differ.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Undifferentiated endocrine symptom documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Continuous glucose monitor review
family_manifest.population: People with diabetes using CGM; age, pregnancy, treatment, caregiver role and device type require individual review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Diabetes technology review in outpatient or specialist care.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Cortisol result discussion documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults or children with a cortisol result from an unspecified test and clinical context.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Generic endocrine result discussion.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Diabetes annual review
family_manifest.population: DHA evidence is adult type 2 diabetes; ADA includes broader diabetes populations with recommendation-specific qualifiers. Children, pregnancy and type 1 diabetes require additional review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Outpatient annual diabetes review; DHA evidence is telehealth-specific.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Diabetic eye screening result review
family_manifest.population: People eligible for NHS diabetic eye screening; pregnancy, age, prior results and surveillance status change the pathway.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Screening-result review and pathway handover, not a comprehensive ophthalmic examination.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Erectile dysfunction endocrine review
family_manifest.population: Adult men with erectile-function concerns; the workflow must not be applied to other sexual-function presentations without review.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Endocrine, urology or sexual-medicine review.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Excessive sweating endocrine review
family_manifest.population: Adults or children with focal or generalized excessive sweating; endocrine, medication, infection, malignancy and autonomic contexts differ.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Undifferentiated endocrine symptom review.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Fracture risk documentation
family_manifest.population: Primarily adults over 50 years in the DoH screening population; younger adults, children, pregnancy and secondary bone disease require separate evidence.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Primary or endocrine fracture-risk documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Goiter symptom review
family_manifest.population: Adults, children and young people with thyroid enlargement, subject to age, function, nodularity and compression qualifiers.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Primary or endocrine review of thyroid enlargement symptoms.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Growth hormone concern documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults or children with an unspecified growth-hormone concern; diagnosis, prior treatment and age are not defined.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Generic endocrine documentation.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — HbA1c result review
family_manifest.population: People with diabetes; age, pregnancy, diabetes type, haemoglobin variants, red-cell turnover and comorbidity affect interpretation.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Outpatient HbA1c result review.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Heat intolerance endocrine documentation
family_manifest.population: Adults or children reporting heat intolerance; thyroid, menopause, medication, autonomic, environmental and infection contexts differ.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Undifferentiated endocrine symptom documentation.

family_manifest.population: Premenopausal women with hirsutism; postmenopausal, paediatric, pregnancy and isolated local-hair presentations require separate assessment.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Outpatient hirsutism review by a clinician.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Home glucose log review
family_manifest.population: People using capillary blood-glucose monitoring; DHA evidence is directly applicable only to adults with type 2 diabetes in licensed telehealth services.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Outpatient clinician review of an existing home glucose log.

family_manifest.population: People with clinician-recognised hyperglycaemia; pregnancy, children, type 1 diabetes and acute metabolic decompensation require distinct pathways.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Outpatient follow-up with emergency concern prompts remaining manual.

family_manifest.population: Adults, children and young people with established hyperthyroidism; pregnancy and thyroid cancer require separate guidance.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Primary or specialist outpatient hyperthyroidism follow-up.

family_manifest.population: Partial: ADA recommendations cover people with diabetes broadly, while the DHA source is adult type 2 diabetes and the legacy workflow has no diabetes-type or age restriction.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Partial: exact for event review and clinician-led prevention documentation, not emergency treatment, medicine dosing, or automatic management.

family_manifest.population: Adults, children and young people with established primary hypothyroidism; pregnancy and central hypothyroidism require distinct pathways.
family_manifest.intended_setting: Primary or specialist outpatient hypothyroidism follow-up.

family_manifest.family_name: Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic — Infertility hormone result review
family_manifest.population: Individuals or couples undergoing infertility evaluation; result meaning varies by sex, age, cycle timing, pregnancy and laboratory method.
